skill.name: Pexo AI Video Agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5–120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: Create professional AI-generated short videos from text descriptions using the Pexo video creation platform. Supports product ads, social clips, explainers, and more.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 534 static analysis findings across 13 files (2110 lines). All 349 external_command detections are false positives: standard bash $(...) substitutions in legitimate shell scripts and backtick code blocks in markdown documentation. All 27 network detections reference the documented pexo.ai API and service URLs. All 79 filesystem detections involve the expected config directory (~/.pexo/config) and temp storage (~/.pexo/tmp), both documented in the skill's setup guide. All 25 env_access detections reference PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL, which are required and documented configuration variables. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent found. Risk level set to medium due to the inherent credential handling and network communication required by the skill's purpose — not due to confirmed threats.

medium_findings.title: API Key Stored in Plaintext Config File
medium_findings.description: The skill requires PEXO_API_KEY to be stored in ~/.pexo/config as plaintext. While this is standard practice for CLI tools, it means the API key is readable by any process with filesystem access. The skill documents this clearly and uses a standard Unix hidden directory convention. Confidence: 0.55 — moderate concern given this is expected CLI behavior, not a flaw in the skill design.

medium_findings.title: Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Pattern
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a dangerous combination of shell command execution, network requests, and credential access. After manual evaluation, this pattern is confirmed as TRUE POSITIVE at the pattern level but FALSE POSITIVE for malicious intent. The skill is an API client that must execute commands, make network calls, and use credentials by design. All three capabilities are documented and transparent. Confidence: 0.85 — the pattern exists but is inherent to any API CLI tool; no malicious use was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Multiple markdown documentation files and shell scripts contain hardcoded URLs to pexo.ai and github.com. All URLs are legitimate service endpoints and documentation links for the Pexo platform. Confidence: 0.95 — definitively false positive for malicious intent; these are the expected service URLs.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory Usage for Config and Temp Storage
low_findings.description: The skill uses ~/.pexo/ for configuration and temporary file storage. This follows the standard Unix convention for per-user application data. Both the config path and temp directory are documented in the setup checklist. Confidence: 0.95 — expected and documented behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Over-Triggering on Markdown Code Blocks
low_findings.description: The SETUP-CHECKLIST.md and TROUBLESHOOTING.md reference files contain extensive code examples in fenced markdown code blocks. The static analyzer flagged backtick characters and command patterns within these documentation examples as executable code. These are instructional examples, not live execution paths. Confidence: 0.98 — definitively false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution ($(...)) in Bash Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: All 10 shell scripts in the scripts/ directory use $(...) command substitution extensively for capturing output from curl, jq, date, basename, and other standard Unix utilities. After evaluation, all uses involve fixed command names with controlled arguments — no user input is directly interpolated into command strings. Arguments flow through jq's --arg for safe JSON construction and are validated before use. This pattern is the standard way to capture subprocess output in bash. Verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — safe usage pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Backtick Execution in Markdown Code Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains backtick characters within fenced code blocks that demonstrate shell script usage (e.g., `pexo-upload.sh proj_123 shoes.jpg`). The static analyzer flagged these as Ruby/shell backtick execution, but they appear inside markdown code blocks that are rendered as examples, not executed. SKILL.md is a documentation file read by the AI, not evaluated as a shell script. Verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — documentation code examples, not executable backticks.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection in Shebang Guards
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations including SKILL.md:3 and various scripts. The flagged pattern is the sha256 hash used in YAML frontmatter and bash version detection guards like [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]. No actual cryptographic operations are performed by the skill's code. Verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — static analyzer misidentified YAML frontmatter and bash version checks as weak cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Windows SAM Database Detection (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged a CRITICAL finding for 'Windows SAM database' at SKILL.md:43. The line reads 'You MUST reply to the user in the SAME language they use.' The word 'SAME' was misidentified by the pattern matcher as referencing the Windows SAM registry hive. This is clearly a language instruction for the AI, not a Windows security reference. Verdict: FALSE POSITIVE — pattern matcher error on the word 'SAME'.

content.user_title: Create AI-Powered Short Videos in Minutes
content.value_statement: Producing professional short videos takes hours of editing and expensive software. This skill connects you to the Pexo AI video agent, which handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music — turning your text descriptions into polished videos ready for social media, ads, or presentations.
actual_capabilities: Create AI-generated short videos from 5 to 120 seconds in length
Produce videos in horizontal (16:9), vertical (9:16), and square (1:1) formats
Upload reference images, videos, and audio files for the AI to use in production
Poll and deliver finished videos with download links once production completes
Support revisions by reusing the same project for follow-up requests
Check account credits and API connectivity before starting production
limitations: Video production takes 15 to 20 minutes for a short clip; longer videos need more time
Requires a Pexo API key and active credits on the pexo.ai platform
The AI agent relays messages between the user and Pexo but cannot generate videos directly
Pexo cannot crawl web URLs — files must be downloaded locally before upload

use_cases.title: Social Media Marketer
use_cases.description: Create engaging short videos for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts from product descriptions and photos. Generate platform-optimized vertical content without video editing skills.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and digital marketers

use_cases.title: Small Business Owner
use_cases.description: Produce professional product ads and promotional videos for online stores. Upload product photos and describe the desired style to get a polished video without hiring a video editor.

use_cases.title: Content Creator and Educator
use_cases.description: Generate explainer videos and educational clips from text descriptions. Turn lesson content or product features into visual explanations for students, customers, or audiences.

prompt_templates.title: Simple video request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a short video for my coffee shop. It should feel warm and inviting.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with no uploaded files

prompt_templates.title: Product video with uploaded images
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product ad showcasing our new running shoes. I will upload product photos for you to use.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has product images to include in the video

prompt_templates.title: Social media vertical video
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a TikTok-style vertical video for our summer fashion collection. Use the three product images I uploaded. The video should be energetic, trendy, and about 15 seconds long.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants platform-specific format with specific mood and duration

prompt_templates.title: Detailed explainer with technical requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a 60-second professional explainer video about how our solar panel technology works. The target audience is homeowners considering solar installation. Use a clean, modern style with clear step-by-step visuals. I have uploaded technical diagrams and product photos as reference.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has specific audience, style, and technical requirements

output_examples.input: Make a video for my bakery's new croissant
output_examples.output: Your video request has been submitted to Pexo. Estimated production time is 15-20 minutes. You can track progress at https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123. I will notify you when the video is ready.

output_examples.input: Create a TikTok ad for my sneaker brand with these product images
output_examples.output: I have uploaded your product images and submitted your request to Pexo. The AI is now creating a vertical video optimized for TikTok. You will receive preview options to choose from before the final delivery. Track progress at https://pexo.ai/project/proj_def456

output_examples.input: Revise the video to make it shorter and add more energy
output_examples.output: Your revision request has been sent to Pexo. The AI will update the video with your feedback — a shorter, more energetic version is now in production. Estimated time: 10-15 minutes for revisions. Project page: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

best_practices: Describe the desired video clearly with target audience, mood, and purpose for best AI results
Upload reference images, product photos, or brand assets to give the AI visual context for production
Review preview options when offered and pick the best one before requesting final delivery
anti_patterns: Sending vague requests like 'make something cool' without describing the video purpose, audience, or style
Expecting instant results — video production takes 15 to 20 minutes; do not poll more than once per minute
Adding your own creative details or style guesses to the user's request instead of relaying their exact words to Pexo

faq.question: How long does it take to create a video?
faq.answer: A short video of about 15 seconds takes 15 to 20 minutes to produce. Longer or more complex videos may need more time. The agent will keep you updated with a project tracking link.

faq.question: What file types can I upload for the video?
faq.answer: You can upload images (JPG, PNG, WebP, BMP, TIFF, HEIC), videos (MP4, MOV, AVI), and audio files (MP3, WAV, AAC, M4A, OGG, FLAC). Uploads provide visual reference for the AI video production.

faq.question: Do I need a Pexo account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a Pexo API key and available credits on the pexo.ai platform. Run the setup diagnostic tool to check your configuration and get started.

faq.question: Can I request changes to a completed video?
faq.answer: Yes. Send your revision feedback using the same project ID. Pexo will update the video based on your feedback. Revisions are faster than creating a new video from scratch.

faq.question: What video formats and sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Videos can be created in three aspect ratios: horizontal (16:9), vertical (9:16 for TikTok and Reels), and square (1:1). Video length can be 5 to 120 seconds.

faq.question: What do I need installed before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need curl, jq, and the file command installed on your system. Run the pexo-doctor.sh diagnostic script to check that everything is configured correctly.
